channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against Open AI
item.description: Mr Musk accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

media:group.media:description: Elon Musk has lost his case against Sam Altman, OpenAI and Microsoft.

item.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure
item.description: One of the men was detained by a zoo keeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

media:group.media:description: Baby Japanese macaque Punch, left, went viral earlier this year for his obsession with a stuffed orangutan.

item.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told
item.description: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

media:group.media:description: Romanian nationals Nandito Badea and George Stana are accused of stabbing the Iran International journalist Pouria Zeraati near his home in Wimbledon in March 2024.

item.title: Spanish PM's socialist party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold
item.description: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sanchez's government could suffer an electoral wipeout in a nationwide vote.

media:group.media:description: The Socialist party of Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has suffered multiple electoral setbacks in recent months.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks for disinfection
item.description: The MV Hondius and its crew has docked at the port of Rotterdam in the Netherlands.

media:group.media:description: A person wearing protective equipment throws bedding next to the cruise ship MV Hondius, where authorities are preparing quarantine arrangements.

item.title: Man arrested after woman and two children found dead in Sydney home
item.description: A man, 47, has been arrested after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police believe is a domestic violence-related incident.

media:group.media:description: Three people have been found dead in a home in Campbelltown.

item.title: Stalking accusations against WA police assistant commissioner, trial begins
item.description: The court hears WA police assistant commissioner Gailene Hamilton allegedly made the complainant — a subordinate officer — feel "smothered" and used a restricted access computer system to check on her location.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Gailene Hamilton's trial in Perth begins over alleged stalking.

item.title: Divers to begin 'demanding' rescue of bodies in underwater cave in Maldives
item.description: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: Monica Montefalcone (left) and daughter Giorgia Sommacal were among the five divers.

item.title: NT police officer on trial for allegedly attempting to pervert course of justice
item.description: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Karol Jarentowski (centre) is on trial in the NT Supreme Court and has pleaded not guilty.

item.title: How netball's 'super' veteran left retirement to break 12-year record
item.description: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Carter

media:group.media:description: Aiken-George walks through the guard of honour in her record-breaking 255th game.

item.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize
item.description: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: "People have said they felt it was eye-opening to realise there are whole parts of your nation's story that have not had the attention they deserve," Wright says.

item.title: Man charged with murder following discovery of body in Hobart home
item.description: A 56-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of a woman was found in a Hobart residence over the weekend.

media:group.media:description: Police say it is believed the woman had been dead for several weeks before her body was discovered over the weekend.

item.title: After another fatal shark attack, experts say risk can never be eliminated
item.description: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Perpitch

media:group.media:description: After another fatal shark attack, experts say the risk can never be eliminated.

item.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say
item.description: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

media:group.media:description: Australians Sam Woripa Watson (left) and Anny Mokotow (middle) who are crew members of the flotilla.

item.title: Free public transport could revive Sydney's live music scene, inquiry hears
item.description: An inquiry into the state of live music in NSW hears from industry stakeholders looking to service younger audiences sprawled across Greater Sydney.
item.dc:creator: Nandini Dhir

media:group.media:description: An inquiry is looking into the state of the live music scene in NSW.

item.title: Scott Pendlebury not looking too far into future as record looms
item.description: Collingwood champion Pendlebury will break the AFL games record against West Coast but hasn't pondered his future beyond then.

media:group.media:description: Scott Pendlebury is set to break the all time appearance record for the AFL/VFL.

item.title: US conducts additional strikes against IS militants in Nigeria
item.description: The US Africa Command announced the strikes days after Trump claimed to have killed the group's second-in-command.

media:group.media:description: Drone footage shared to social media by AFRICOM shows coordinated strikes against Islamic State militants in Nigeria.

item.title: Blanchett says #MeToo reckoning in Hollywood 'got killed very quickly'
item.description: The Oscar-winning actor says Hollywood's reckoning over sexual misconduct and gender equality has stalled despite the global impact of #MeToo.

media:group.media:description: Cate Blanchett at the 79th international film festival in Cannes, France.

item.title: Queensland man accused of paying to watch live child sexual abuse
item.description: Police allege the 38-year-old from the state's far north took part in more than 40 live shows on an online platform. WARNING: This story contains details of child sexual abuse.

media:group.media:description: Anti-child exploitation officers searched a home in Innisfail in January 2025.

item.title: Australia suspends new private college bids to enrol international students
item.description: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Labor is implementing a 12-month pause on new private colleges and training organisations applying to offer courses to international students.

item.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts
item.description: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".
item.dc:creator: Angelique Donnellan and Adam Langenberg

media:group.media:description: Global company Nyrstar says its metal smelter in Port Pirie, South Australia has been losing tens of millions of dollars each month.

item.title: 'Predator' arsonist's $1.5m fire an 'act of retribution', court told
item.description: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: The fire was visible from nearby buildings.

item.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address
item.description: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

media:group.media:description: Premier Peter Malinauskas says most of the golf course protesters have conducted themselves civilly.

item.title: Port Kembla identified as preferred AUKUS base in secret report
item.description: A labour union has called for transparency after Port Kembla in NSW was identified as a preferred east coast location for a nuclear submarine base in newly released documents.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Under the AUKUS arrangement, Australia is set to buy several nuclear-powered Virginia-class submarines from the US in the 2030s.

item.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull
item.description: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tom Robinson

media:group.media:description: A planned cull of 20,000 native birds at Newcastle Waters cattle station in the NT will no longer be going ahead.
